topic: Cost of Sales and Inventory Management
subtopic: Impact of Inventory on Financial Statements
title: Impact of Inventory on Financial Statements: If the ending inventory is understated, what effect does this...
summary: Analyze a financial accounting concept related to Impact of Inventory on Financial Statements.
prompt: If the ending inventory is understated, what effect does this have on the cost of goods sold?

options.text: cost of goods sold (COGS) is overstated

solution: Understating ending inventory leads to an overstatement of cost of goods sold (COGS) because COGS is calculated as Beginning Inventory + Purchases - Ending Inventory. Thus, when ending inventory is lower, the resulting COGS will be higher, which negatively impacts the net income reported in the financial statements. This concept is crucial for accurate financial reporting and analysis.
